channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft: Windows 11 users can't access C: drive on some Samsung PCs
item.description: Microsoft is investigating a new issue affecting some Samsung laptops running Windows 11 after installing the February 2026 security updates, in which users lose access to their C:\ drive and are unab...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI seeks victims of Steam games used to spread malware
item.description: The FBI is asking gamers who installed Steam titles containing malware to provide information as part of an ongoing investigation into eight malicious games uploaded to the gaming platform. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake PoCs, Misunderstood Risks Cause Cisco SD-WAN Chaos
item.description: The excitement around Cisco's latest SD-WAN bugs has inspired some light fraud, misunderstandings, and overlooked risks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Data Gap: Why Nonprofit Cyber Incidents Go Underreported
item.description: Threat actors target nonprofits due to security gaps and highly coveted information, but a lack of sufficient data makes it difficult to grasp the entire picture....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Southeast Asian Militaries with AppleChris and MemFun Malware
item.description: A suspected China-based cyber espionage operation has targeted Southeast Asian military organizations as part of a state-sponsored campaign that dates back to at least 2020. Palo Alto Networks Unit 42...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cyberattackers Don't Care About Good Causes
item.description: Sightline Security's founder and advisory board discuss how cybersecurity poses significant problems for nonprofits and suggest ways the industry can help....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Poland's nuclear research centre targeted by cyberattack
item.description: Poland's National Centre for Nuclear Research (NCBJ) says hackers targeted its IT infrastructure, but the attack was detected and blocked before causing any impact. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Meta to Shut Down Instagram End-to-End Encrypted Chat Support Starting May 2026
item.description: Meta has announced plans to discontinue support for end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for chats on Instagram after May 8, 2026. "If you have chats that are impacted by this change, you will see instruction...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft investigates classic Outlook sync and connection issues
item.description: ​Microsoft is investigating several issues causing email synchronization and connection problems when using the classic Outlook desktop client. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Will AI Save Consumers From Smartphone-Based Phishing Attacks?
item.description: Sophisticated phishing attacks are bypassing on-device protections with troubling frequency, making it more critical than ever for users to protect themselves from potential threats, new research from...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: INTERPOL Dismantles 45,000 Malicious IPs, Arrests 94 in Global Cybercrime
item.description: INTERPOL on Friday announced the takedown of 45,000 malicious IP addresses and servers used in connection with phishing, malware, and ransomware campaigns, as part of the agency's ongoing efforts to d...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: From VMware to what’s next: Protecting data during hypervisor migration
item.description: Hypervisor migrations can introduce hidden risks that threaten data availability and recovery. Acronis explains why verified backups and cross-platform recovery are essential during VMware transitions...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Storm-2561 Spreads Trojan VPN Clients via SEO Poisoning to Steal Credentials
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a credential theft campaign that employs fake virtual private network (VPN) clients distributed through search engine optimization (SEO) poisoning techniques. "The c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Police sinkholes 45,000 IP addresses in cybercrime crackdown
item.description: An international law enforcement action codenamed "Operation Synergia III" has sinkholed tens of thousands of IP addresses and seized servers linked to cybercrime operations worldwide. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Investigating a New Click-Fix Variant
item.description: Disclaimer: This report has been prepared by the Threat Research Center to enhance cybersecurity awareness and support the strengthening of defense capabilities. It is based on independent research an...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fake enterprise VPN sites used to steal company credentials
item.description: A threat actor tracked as Storm-2561 is distributing fake enterprise VPN clients from Ivanti, Cisco, and Fortinet to steal VPN credentials from unsuspecting users. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Most Google Cloud Attacks Start With Bug Exploitation
item.description: Forget stolen credentials and misconfigurations; AI means vulnerability exploits that beat patching cycles are the top cause of compromises in the cloud....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Real-Time Banking Trojan Strikes Brazil's Pix Users
item.description: The latest banking Trojan campaign to hit Brazil combines classic malware with a real-time human operator, waiting for the perfect moment to strike....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google Fixes Two Chrome Zero-Days Exploited in the Wild Affecting Skia and V8
item.description: Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address two high-severity vulnerabilities that it said have been exploited in the wild. The list of vulnerabilities is as fol...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Nine CrackArmor Flaws in Linux AppArmor Enable Root Escalation, Bypass Container Isolation
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities within the Linux kernel's AppArmor module that could be exploited by unprivileged users to circumvent kernel protections, esc...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Starbucks discloses data breach affecting hundreds of employees
item.description: Starbucks has disclosed a data breach affecting hundreds of employees after threat actors gained access to their Starbucks Partner Central accounts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google fixes two new Chrome zero-days exploited in attacks
item.description: Google has released emergency security updates to patch two high-severity Chrome vulnerabilities exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Authorities Disrupt SocksEscort Proxy Botnet Exploiting 369,000 IPs Across 163 Countries
item.description: A court-authorized international law enforcement operation has dismantled a criminal proxy service named SocksEscort that enslaved thousands of residential routers worldwide into a botnet for committi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Veeam Patches 7 Critical Backup & Replication Flaws Allowing Remote Code Execution
item.description: Veeam has released security updates to address multiple critical vulnerabilities in its Backup & Replication software that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution. The vulner...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Can't Wait
item.description: Organizations have to prepare to ensure they have cryptography in place in the post-quantum world....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Canadian retail giant Loblaw notifies customers of data breach
item.description: Still, out of an abundance of caution, Loblaw says it has automatically logged out all customers from their accounts. Account holders who need to access the company's digital services will have to log...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iran MOIS Colludes With Criminals to Boost Cyberattacks
item.description: Iranian APTs have long pretended to be cybercriminal groups. Now they're working with actual cybercriminal groups....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: England Hockey investigating ransomware data breach
item.description: England Hockey, the governing body for field hockey in England, is investigating a potential data breach after the AiLock ransomware gang listed it as a victim on its data leak site. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Commercial Spyware Opponents Fear US Policy Shifting
item.description: Rescinded sanctions and reactivated contracts have created confusion about the Trump administration's spyware policy and where it draws the line....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI-generated Slopoly malware used in Interlock ransomware attack
item.description: A new malware strain dubbed Slopoly, likely created using generative AI tools, allowed a threat actor to remain on a compromised server for more than a week and steal data in an Interlock ransomware a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
